name: Frances' Peanut Butter Treats
recipeIngredient: 2 lbs creamy or chunky peanut butter
2/3 bar paraffin wax, the kind used for canning
24 oz. chocolate chips, milk or semi-sweet
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together butter and peanut butter. Add confectioners sugar slowly and mix thoroughly.
2. Roll mixture into small balls (no more than an inch per piece) and place a toothpick in the center.
3. Melt chocolate chips, then add melted wax. Stir well until smooth. This can be done using the microwave or on the stove top. In the microwave, use glass dishes for best results, and melt wax slowly. On the stove top, use a heavy saucepan and melt slowly to avoid burning the chocolate or wax.
4. Dip peanut butter balls into chocolate and swirl around to ensure they are completely covered.
5. Let candy set on a sheet of wax paper. Store in refrigerator. These are perfect for serving with coffee and dessert, and I include them in boxes of homemade assorted cookies for gift giving.
Note: The original recipe text included attribution lines and notes that have been removed as per the instructions.
